title: autobench.improver.prediction.refuted_live v1
description: Live (partial) refutation of a previously-emitted autobench.improver.prediction.v1 fired the moment incoming iter N+1 cases make the prediction mathematically unachievable, instead of waiting for the iter N+1 → N+2 boundary. Emitted at most once per prediction. Source: /autobench.

session_id.description: ULID for the autobench run (matches the prediction event)

iteration.description: RSI iteration being verified live, i.e. iter N+1 (one after the iteration that produced the prediction)

prediction.description: The original Prediction payload being live-refuted

actuals_so_far.description: Verdict-count histogram of iter N+1 cases completed at refutation time

remaining_cases.description: Number of iter N+1 cases not yet dispatched at refutation time

is_refuted.description: True when the prediction is mathematically unachievable given actuals_so_far + remaining_cases

refutation_reason.description: Human-readable explanation citing the unreachable verdict-delta entries

confidence_at_refute.description: Original prediction confidence carried forward at refutation time; null when not refuted
